question:
Q.1 In the following sentence, four words are underlined, out of which one word is incorrectly spelt. Identify the INCORRECTLY spelt word.

Seema was a timid girl. She did not talk to people who were not familiar to her. She was an introvert since adolosense.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: ✍️ Spelling Check:

• **Incorrect**: adolosense.
• **Correct**: **Adolescence** (A-D-O-L-E-S-C-E-N-C-E) (ಕಿಶೋರಾವಸ್ಥೆ). ✅
• **Familiar**: ಚಿರಪರಿಚಿತ.
• **Timid**: ಹೇಡಿ/ನಾಚಿಕೆ ಸ್ವಭಾವದ.

question: Q.2 Select the most appropriate idiom/phrase that can substitute the underlined segment: **Vennela made a big fuss about a small problem.**
options: Get a taste of your own medicine
explanation:
Mastery Insight: ☕ Idiom Meanings:

• **A storm in a teacup**: Great anger or excitement about a trivial matter (ಸಣ್ಣ ವಿಷಯಕ್ಕೆ ದೊಡ್ಡ ಹಂಗಾಮಾ ಮಾಡುವುದು) - ✅.
• **Barking up the wrong tree**: Looking in the wrong place.
• **Add insult to injury**: Making a bad situation worse.

question: Q.3 Select the grammatically correct statement from among the given options.
options: It is a nice party. I am enjoying the party.
It is a party. I am enjoying an party.
It is party. I am enjoying a party.
It is the party. I am enjoying an party.
answer: It is a nice party. I am enjoying the party.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🥳 Article Usage:

• First mention: 'A nice party' (General).
• Second mention: '**The** party' (Specific reference to the same party).
• Option B and D are wrong because 'party' starts with a consonant, so it cannot take 'an'. ✅

question: Q.4 Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word: **The Emperor was considered as a wicked man.**
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 😈 **Wicked** means evil or morally wrong (ದುಷ್ಟ).

• **Virtuous**: Having high moral standards (ಪುಣ್ಯವಂತ/ಸಚ್ಚಾರಿತ್ರ್ಯವುಳ್ಳ) - ✅ Antonym.
• **Vile**: Extremely unpleasant/wicked (Synonym).
• **Savage**: Fierce or violent.

question:
Q.5 Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph.

A. We often make all things around us the way we want them.
B. Even during our pilgrimages, we have begun to look for whatever makes our heart happy, gives comfort to our body and peace to the mind.
C. Our mind is resourceful enough to find shortcuts in simple and easy ways.
D. It is as if external solutions will fulfil our needs, and we do not want to make any special efforts even in our spiritual search.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🧩 Logical Flow:

• **A**: General statement about controlling our surroundings.
• **B**: Specific example of this behavior during pilgrimages.
• **D**: The result—looking for external comfort rather than internal effort.
• **C**: Conclusion about the mind finding shortcuts. ✅

question: Q.6 Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: **Sharp practice**
explanation: Mastery Insight: ⚖️ **Sharp practice** refers to dishonest or barely legal business dealings (ವಂಚನೆ/ಅಪ್ರಾಮಾಣಿಕ ವ್ಯವಹಾರ). ✅

question: Q.7 Select the one-word substitute for: **A book or set of books giving information about all areas of knowledge**
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 📚 Books:

• **Encyclopedia**: A comprehensive reference work (ವಿಶ್ವಕೋಶ) - ✅.
• **Anthology**: A collection of poems or stories.
• **Dictionary**: List of words and meanings.

question: Q.8 Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: **Matching**
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 👯 **Matching** means looking similar or corresponding (ಹೋಲುವಂತಹ).

• **Resembling**: To be like or similar to (ಹೋಲುವ) - ✅ Synonym.

question: Q.9 Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: **Derision**
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🗣️ **Derision** means contemptuous ridicule or mockery (ಅವಹೇಳನ/ಗೇಲಿ).

• **Admiration**: Respect and warm approval (ಮೆಚ್ಚುಗೆ/ಗೌರವ) - ✅ Antonym.
• **Termination**: Ending something.

question: Q.10 Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: **Peruse**
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 📖 **Peruse** means to read or examine something carefully (ಪರಿಶೀಲಿಸು/ಓದು).

• **Check**: To examine for accuracy or condition - ✅ Closest Synonym.
• **Pursue**: To follow or chase.

question: Q.11 Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word: **The salesman yielded to the demands of his corrupt boss.**
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🏳️ **Yielded** means to surrender or give in (ಒಪ್ಪಿಕೊಂಡನು/ಶರಣಾದನು).

• **Conceded**: To admit or agree to something after first denying it (ಸೋಲೊಪ್ಪಿಕೊಳ್ಳುವುದು/ಒಪ್ಪಿಕೊಳ್ಳುವುದು) - ✅ Synonym.

question: Q.12 Select the one-word substitute for: **A person who does not believe that God or Gods exist.**
explanation:
Mastery Insight: ⚛️ Beliefs:

• **Atheist**: A person who disbelieves in God (ನಾಸ್ತಿಕ) - ✅.
• **Blasphemous**: Sacrilegious against God or sacred things.

question:
Q.13 Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph.

A. It’s because the ‘practical people’ in society boast of having a practical approach to life.
B. It is very likely for youngsters to seek advice from their elders.
C. Emphasising upon the times that prevailed, the youth very much get their queries answered systematically.
D. In the similar context even the elders are, in most likelihood, expected to give their advice as a word of guidance.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🧩 Logic:

• **B**: Introduces the action (youngsters seeking advice).
• **D**: Links to the response (elders giving advice).
• **C**: Explains how queries are answered systematically.
• **A**: Provides the reason (practical approach in society). ✅

question:
Q.14 Arrange the sentences in order to form a coherent paragraph.

A. But she has no idea an aunt and uncle are waiting for her.
B. ‘The Strange Child’ is the story of a young girl who believes she is the only one on the planet.
C. Her parents simply vanished into thin air one day.
D. She travels the world in search of Nevada because she aspires to reside in the Mojave Desert.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 📖 Story sequence:

• **B**: Introduces the book and the girl's belief.
• **C**: Explains the background (parents vanishing).
• **D**: Describes her goal/journey.
• **A**: Contrast (the aunt/uncle she doesn't know about). ✅

question: Q.15 Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: ✍️ Rule: 'i' before 'e' except after 'c'.

• **Incorrect**: Reciept.
• **Correct**: **Receipt** (R-E-C-E-I-P-T) (ರಶೀದಿ). ✅

question:
Q.16 Arrange the sentences in the correct order.

A. But the question that perturbs Ruskin’s mind is what social pressure can be exercised against a dishonest person.
B. The merchant, for instance, must supply perfect and pure things to the people.
C. Ruskin believes that in every civilised society there exist five intellectual professions, namely the soldier, the pastor, the physician, the lawyer and the merchant.
D. Persons belonging to these professions are expected to perform their duty honestly.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 💼 Logic:

• **C**: Intro to the five professions.
• **D**: General expectation of honesty from them.
• **B**: Specific example (the merchant).
• **A**: The 'But' statement posing a critical question. ✅

question: Q.17 Identify the segment with a grammatical error: **The availability of funds will be / ensured if they all tried to / submit the proposals on time.**
options: ensured if they all tried to
The availability of funds will be
answer: ensured if they all tried to
explanation:
Mastery Insight: ⏳ Conditionals (Mixed Tense Error):

• The first part is 'will be' (Future).
• The 'if' clause should be in present simple for a real possibility.
• **Correction**: 'if they all **try** to'. ✅

question: Q.18 Select the most appropriate synonym for: **moderate**
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 💰 **Moderate** means average in amount or intensity (ಮಧ್ಯಮ/ಸಮಂಜಸ).

• **Reasonable**: Fair and sensible (ಸಮಂಜಸವಾದ) - ✅ Synonym.

question: Q.19 Select the passive voice: **Did you not buy the return tickets to Dehradun?**
options: Had the return tickets to Dehradun not bought by you?
Were the return tickets to Dehradun not being bought by you?
Was the return tickets to Dehradun not bought by you?
Were the return tickets to Dehradun not bought by you?
answer: Were the return tickets to Dehradun not bought by you?
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 📢 Active to Passive (Simple Past Question):

• **Active**: Did + Subject + V1?
• **Passive**: Was/Were + Object + V3?
• 'Tickets' is plural, so we use **'Were'**. ✅

question: Q.20 Passive voice for: **Mahesh and his team were helping the fire and rescue team.**
options: The fire and rescue team was being helped by Mahesh and his team.
The fire and rescue team helped by Mahesh and his team.
The fire and rescue team being help by Mahesh and his team.
Mahesh and his team were been helping the fire and rescue team.
answer: The fire and rescue team was being helped by Mahesh and his team.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 📢 Past Continuous Passive:

• **Rule**: was/were + **being** + V3.
• 'Team' is singular, so we use 'was being helped'. ✅

question:
Cloze Test: Learning a new language can be challenging, but it's also (1) _________. It can open up new opportunities for communication...

 Select for blank 1.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🏅 Positive Context:

• Since it opens opportunities, the experience is **rewarding** (ಫಲಪ್ರದ/ಪ್ರತಿಫಲ ನೀಡುವ). ✅

question:
Cloze Test: When learning a new language, it's important to be patient and (2) _________ yourself time to progress.

 Select for blank 2.
explanation: Mastery Insight: ⏳ To succeed, you must **give** (ನೀಡು) yourself time. ✅

question:
Cloze Test: One way to make language learning easier is to (3) _________ to it every day, even if it's just for a few minutes.

 Select for blank 3.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🤝 Consistency:

• To **commit** (ಬದ್ಧರಾಗಿರಿ) to a task means to dedicate yourself to it daily. ✅

question:
Cloze Test: By immersing yourself in the language and culture, you can accelerate your (4) _________ and gain a deeper understanding.

 Select for blank 4.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 📈 Improvement:

• Practice accelerates your **progress** (ಪ್ರಗತಿ). ✅

question:
Cloze Test: Learning a new language is not only practical, but it's also (5) _________ and can be a fun experience.

 Select for blank 5.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🎉 Fun context:

• The blank is followed by 'fun experience', so **enjoyable** (ಆನಂದದಾಯಕ) is the correct fit. ✅
